title: trichilia elegans subsp. elegans
intro: Trichilia elegans subsp. elegans, commonly known as the African star apple, is a subspecies of plant in the family Meliaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Trichilia elegans subsp. elegans include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be made by boiling the leaves and roots in water, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
